methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Stellaria is a seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Nicolas Breyton and backed by CEA and Schneider Electric, which has raised €23M to date. The nuclear energy sector comp universe consists of 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers, including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Applied Atomics.

The peer-median valuation method proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy sector. A search across the 4 same-sector peers identified only 1 adjacent-stage datapoint, falling short of the 3-company minimum required for a reliable median anchor. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the sector-specific capital intensity and regulatory environment of nuclear development.

Without a viable triangulation method supported by adequate comparable data, a valuation range cannot be defensibly established from this analysis. The primary uncertainty is the absence of sufficient same-stage or near-stage nuclear peers with disclosed valuation metrics. Proceeding with valuation would require either direct DCF modeling of Stellaria's specific capital requirements and timeline to commercialization, or expansion of the comp set to include earlier or later-stage nuclear peers with explicit justification for stage-adjustment factors. Additional diligence on the top 5 private peers' recent funding rounds and implied valuations is recommended before triangulation can be attempted.
